rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts (Plan mode = Shift+Tab cycle or /plan, NOT Shift+P).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: File uses 'Shift+Tab until the status bar shows plan' — matches canonical fact. No Shift+P drift.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Session widget 'Module 2 - Plan mode done right' + 'Start a new Claude Code session at your repo root' uses correct vocabulary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No as-is imports from the internet — fork + LICENSE + attribution.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block names the fork path (curriculum/skills/external/pocock-skills/grill-me/), LICENSE preservation, commit SHA, MIT license. Fork directory exists on disk (SKILL.md present).
P2 prompt closes with 'My task:' open-hook — correct per rule 1d (student input genuinely required: their backlog task).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All four prompts carry semantic lead-ins (plan, walk down branches, name the design pattern, auto-load query).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape: lead-in, label, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All prompts use `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` label, blank line, fenced block. No custom labels.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No skill invocations inside prompt fences. Grill-me skill referenced in maintainer block as design source only.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Preamble before action — design around it (4+ sub-steps or plan-mode trigger).
rules_evaluated.evidence: P2 prompt (Phase 2) invokes plan mode and names 4+ sub-steps (files touched, shape of the change, tests, what to check before done) — rule 17 structural trigger fires. No in-fence 'No preamble' suppressor or body primer immediately after the fence cuing student to skim past Claude's narration.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add either an in-fence final line ('No preamble. Begin reading the codebase.') or a body primer right after the fence ('Claude will narrate before reading; skim past the opening sentence and look for the plan file path.'). Non-blocking — pattern, not correctness violation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't restate session CWD inside prompt body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No absolute paths or cd commands inside fences.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No module-name references in prompt prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses 'next module's concern' (not 'M3'); prompts contain no M1-M8 tokens; maintainer-block M-references are exempt.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block 'Frameworks riffed on' bullet: mattpocock/skills grill-me at https://github.com/mattpocock/skills/blob/main/grill-me/SKILL.md returned 404; raw.githubusercontent equivalent also 404. Repo root (github.com/mattpocock/skills) is alive but the grill-me/SKILL.md path no longer resolves at commit a6bdfd9 on main.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Re-verify the skill path on mattpocock/skills (may have moved, been renamed, or commit a6bdfd9 is no longer reachable on main). Either update the URL to a live commit-pinned permalink, point at the forked copy at curriculum/skills/external/pocock-skills/grill-me/, or mark [UNVERIFIED ATTRIBUTION] until resolved. Non-blocking because the claim sits in maintainer-block attribution prose, not in student-copied content, and the forked-copy exists in-repo (preserving the attribution chain).
All cited dates (2026-04-22 permission-modes, 2026-04-21 Klaassen run) within window.
No round-number AI failure stats in this file.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Shift+Tab until the status bar shows plan
Claude Code's plan directory (auto, descriptive filename)

citations_checked.claim: Roger Martin 'What would have to be true' (Playing to Win)
